direction.title: Guaranteeing downstream performance of resource-efficient small models
direction.rationale: Both trees show recent actions shifting away from scaling towards efficiency (objective shift in T_0, data curation in T_1). The collective trajectory is towards resource-efficient methods, but the endpoint lacks a mechanism to ensure that the resulting small model retains needed capabilities without relying on large resources.
direction.trend: Shift from scaling-law-based model development to resource-efficient specialization through downscaling or data selection.
direction.trend_endpoint: A set of theoretically grounded methods that produce small specialized models without reliance on large external resources.
direction.implied_next_problem: How to validate performance guarantees empirically when existing benchmarks assume large model capabilities? Need structural property of 'resource-agnostic performance certification'.
direction.non_obvious_property: Property: performance certification that does not assume access to a large evaluation set or a large teacher for comparison. Obvious composition that fails: use a held-out test set from the target domain, which may not be available in scarce data scenarios. Prior work (e.g., scaling laws) assumes large compute for validation. No prior work establishes a bound on downstream performance given only a tiny seed dataset and no external models.

per_trend_predictions.direction: We propose a certification protocol that evaluates small specialized models on a set of synthetic probes generated by linearly interpolating between training inputs. The probes cover the representation space due to a convexity property ensured by the data selection method. A linear error predictor trained on these probes provides provable performance guarantees for any test input, without requiring large external benchmarks.
per_trend_predictions.rationale: The trend endpoint establishes theoretically grounded methods for building small specialized models. However, validating their performance remains a bottleneck because existing benchmarks assume large model capabilities. Our method leverages the convex representation set induced by the data selection process to enable resource-agnostic empirical certification.
per_trend_predictions.builds_on: Need a Small Specialized Language Model? Plan Early!
per_trend_predictions.persistent_gap: How to empirically validate performance guarantees of small specialized models without relying on large external benchmarks.
per_trend_predictions.why_still_open: Prior work either uses benchmarks designed for large models (which are inappropriate for small models) or relies on confidence scores that are uncalibrated. No existing method provides provable performance certification using only the small model itself.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_keep: The data selection method that ensures the small model's training representations form a convex set.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_remove: Reliance on large external benchmarks for validation.
per_trend_predictions.bridge_add: Generate synthetic probe inputs by linearly interpolating between randomly selected training inputs. Train a linear model to predict the small model's error on a probe from its representation. For a new input, compute its representation, predict error using the linear model, and certify if the predicted error is below a threshold. The convexity property guarantees the linear interpolation covers the entire representation space and the error function is linear on that set.
per_trend_predictions.non_obvious_property: Property: Convexity of the small model's representation set in the training data. Why prior work cannot exploit it: Previous methods (e.g., L2 robustness certificates) assume Lipschitz continuity but not convex representation sets, and cannot use simple linear interpolation to create a complete probe set. Why composition fails: A naive composition of 'random interpolation + linear regression' would not guarantee coverage without the convexity property; interpolated points might lie outside the manifold where the error function is linear, making the certificate invalid.
per_trend_predictions.obvious_composition_check: Random interpolation of training inputs + linear regression for error prediction. The load-bearing piece that makes this non-obvious is the convexity of the representation set, which ensures that linear interpolation spans the entire space and that the error function is linear on that set. Without this property, the composition would not yield a valid certificate. Thus the short composition omits the essential structural assumption.
per_trend_predictions.forest_implied_next_problem: How to validate performance guarantees empirically when existing benchmarks assume large model capabilities? Need structural property of 'resource-agnostic performance certification'.
